raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize (A) high-level conceptual framing, logical consistency, and section-level structure, or (B) sentence-level writing polish and copyediting?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section asserts a central contribution and frames the problem, so reviewing the clarity and coherence of the core thesis and gap is essential.",
    "R1.2": "Definitions and relationships (e.g., two-level control, p(t)=c(t)-d(t), objectives and constraints) should be checked for internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.5": "Section-level flow and ordering—from problem setup to contribution and method—may need assessment for narrative coherence.",
    "R1.6": "Technical prose may require sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments for readability.",
    "RX.1": "Ambiguity about the desired review depth and limited context warrant explicit acknowledgment of uncertainties and assumptions.",
    "RX.2": "Comments should avoid endorsing unsubstantiated performance or novelty claims beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The review must stay within the scope of this section and the specified focus once clarified.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should avoid suggesting practices or claims that could lead to unsafe or irresponsible grid operations or misinterpretation.",
    "RX.5": "Any observations should be traceable to specific phrases or statements in the provided text to maintain provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize (A) high-level conceptual framing, logical consistency, and section-level structure, or (B) sentence-level writing polish and copyediting?

rationale.R1.1: The section asserts a central contribution and frames the problem, so reviewing the clarity and coherence of the core thesis and gap is essential.
rationale.R1.2: Definitions and relationships (e.g., two-level control, p(t)=c(t)-d(t), objectives and constraints) should be checked for internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.5: Section-level flow and ordering—from problem setup to contribution and method—may need assessment for narrative coherence.
rationale.R1.6: Technical prose may require sentence-level clarity, concision, and tone adjustments for readability.
rationale.RX.1: Ambiguity about the desired review depth and limited context warrant explicit acknowledgment of uncertainties and assumptions.
rationale.RX.2: Comments should avoid endorsing unsubstantiated performance or novelty claims beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The review must stay within the scope of this section and the specified focus once clarified.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid suggesting practices or claims that could lead to unsafe or irresponsible grid operations or misinterpretation.
rationale.RX.5: Any observations should be traceable to specific phrases or statements in the provided text to maintain provenance.
